_comment: Per-arm eval fixture (ADR-077 D5). Cases bind ARM-SPECIFIC partitions so recall is measurable per routing arm (Flash-Lite vs Flash) — on the single shared 'claude-infra-v2' partition, per-arm recall is a category error. ARM-KEYING MECHANISM (ADR-077 D1): arms are keyed by the ContextVar routing-partition primitive (`_WRITE_CTX` in graphiti_write.py:56/120, set with {group_id,name,content_hash} before the nested add_episode) — OR a documented time-windowed partition fallback — NOT a naive `group_id` kwarg on the nested `resolve_edge`. ADR-077 D1 records that graphiti-core 0.28.1 telemetry shows that kwarg dropped (group_id=null on resolve_edge), so it is unusable as a partition primitive; live propagation confirmation is an explore precondition carried to the downstream consumer (ADR-097). The harness keys arms read-side via the partition's `-arm-<x>` suffix (graphiti-read.py already honors `WHERE r.group_id = $g`). Cases hand-authored; no document bodies written by this path.

context: What is the v2 substrate engine built on, and what is the only durable runtime state?
